data.location: 9202 Jeff Fuqua Blvd, Orlando, FL 32827
data.status: Targeting August opening (construction underway; timeline pending buildout)
data.short_description: A hybrid airport concept branded as Zaza Bar and Cuban Cafe combining full-service dining, coffee, bar, and grab-and-go in ~1,500 sq ft. Founded by Ruben Perez with CEO Luis Fernandez. Planned daily 5 a.m.–9 p.m.

data.location: 1921 US-92, Auburndale, FL 33823
data.short_description: A 4,500-square-foot restaurant with dine-in seating for ~75 guests, patio, and drive-thru. Owner Nathan Devine's sixth location managed by operator Scott Hunt. Planned Sun–Thu 10 a.m.–10 p.m.; Fri–Sat 10 a.m.–11 p.m.

data.location: 2610 S Ferncreek Ave, Orlando, FL 32806
data.status: Targeting early April opening (pending final approvals)
data.short_description: First full-service location for the brand with ~78 indoor seats and additional outdoor seating, expanding beyond takeout. Menu features oxtails, curry goat, and jerk chicken.

data.location: 6536 Unit H Stadium Drive, Zephyrhills, FL 33542
data.short_description: A walk-up shaved ice shop with no indoor seating, operated by owners Darrell Lake Valencia and Nikki Lake with family involvement. Starting menu includes shaved ice, popcorn, soda, and dirty lemonades with plans to expand to smoothies and boba teas.

data.location: 220 N. Rome Ave., Tampa, FL 33603
data.short_description: A full-service Spanish restaurant from Tampa group Tastes Pretty Good seating about 125 guests, centered on tapas, paella, and wood-fired cooking. Features a custom-built wood-fired grill imported from Spain as a kitchen centerpiece.

data.location: 4521 Southside Boulevard, Jacksonville, FL 32216
data.status: Targeting an October or November opening
data.short_description: A conveyor belt-style full-service dining concept seating 150–180 guests, serving sushi, hibachi, ramen, appetizers, milk tea, and alcoholic beverages. Owner Ivan Lu also owns other Jacksonville-area restaurants including Tonka.

data.location: 2740 Greenbriar Pkwy SW, Atlanta, GA 30311
data.short_description: A counter-service BBQ restaurant marking the brand's first brick-and-mortar, transitioning from pop-ups and a food truck since September 2023. Signature items include smoked oxtails, brisket, and ribs; initially open Friday–Sunday until sellout.

data.location: 1048 Copperfield Blvd NE Suite 101, Concord, NC 28025
data.event_date: Soft opening mid-May; grand opening late May or early June 2026
data.short_description: A cigar-focused lounge with a bar and indoor golf simulator, owned by Bjorn Mohaugen. Targeting a soft opening in mid-May followed by a grand opening in late May or early June.

data.location: 3661 Concord Parkway South, Concord, NC 28027
data.short_description: Opening in the former Smoothie King location, owned by Sarah and Barrett Dabbs who also own Johnny Roger's BBQ & Burgers in the same shopping plaza. No opening date or timeline was provided in the article.

data.location: 10610 Centrum Pkwy Suite L, Pineville, NC 28134

data.status: Targeting an opening in about one to one-and-a-half months
data.short_description: A Bengali-focused takeout concept with catering, frozen goods, and a halal butcher counter, owned by Sabyasachi Patra. Pending remaining buildout progress as of article date.

data.location: 4502 Fountain Drive, Wilmington, NC 28403
data.status: The team is targeting a late spring to early summer opening as construction continues
data.short_description: A coastal taco concept opening a new standalone location near UNC Wilmington, co-owned by Steve Christian. Construction is ongoing as of article date.

data.location: Apex, NC (full address not specified)
data.short_description: A Florida-based superfood concept expanding to Apex, NC. Full address and specific opening date were not provided in the article excerpt.

data.location: 750 W. Main Street, Bartow, FL 33830

data.status: Targeting a soft opening in May with full opening in June
data.short_description: A fast-casual Thai restaurant expanding from a long-running food truck, owned by Thomas and Prapapan Klug. The food truck will continue operating alongside the new brick-and-mortar space.

data.location: 1037 Dixieland Mall Lane, Lakeland, FL 33803
data.short_description: A hybrid counter-service concept with outdoor patio, TVs, and live music, marking the brand's first Florida location. Owned by CEO Chris Cavalieri and partners, expanding the Buffalo-born brand.

data.location: 3419 E County Rd 542, Lakeland, FL 33801
data.short_description: A full-service Cuban restaurant with indoor seating and a large outdoor event space for live entertainment, owned by Odalis Chanlatte and Isnelys Prieto. Menu features Cuban sandwiches, ropa vieja, oxtail, palomillo steak, and the signature "Riconator."

data.location: 114 Sailors Cove Dr, Port St. Joe, FL 32456
data.short_description: A family-run seafood restaurant, market, and retail concept from the Arreguin family, seating 150–170 guests inside and outside with bay views. Signature items include a seafood boil and a Mediterranean-style fish entree, plus a full bar.

data.location: 750 W. Main Street, Bartow, FL 33830

data.short_description: First brick-and-mortar for the Gin Khao food truck brand, owned by Thomas and Prapapan Klug, with limited seating for ~14 guests in a fast-casual format. The food truck will continue operating 2–3 times per week alongside the new location.

data.location: 1510 Abbotts Creek Cir, Kernersville, NC 27284
data.short_description: A 200-seat all-day breakfast and brunch concept owned by Alex Kazakos, continuing the family-run brand his father founded in 2008. Signature items include banana foster waffles, pancakes, and French toast; planned hours Mon–Sun 6 a.m.–3 p.m.

data.location: 10610 Centrum Pkwy Suite L, Pineville, NC 28134

data.location: 20601 Torrence Chapel Rd Suite 106, Cornelius, NC 28031
data.short_description: A donut shop with a strong takeout focus, specialty square donuts, and a coffee program, owned by Dustin and Colleen Wanemacher who acquired the brand in 2023. Limited indoor and outdoor seating will be available.

data.location: 132 Brevard Ct., Charlotte, NC 28202

data.short_description: A relocated and expanded concept in Brevard Courtyard with a larger footprint, full bar, and upstairs dining, operated by Matt Marenna and team. Lunch menu features fresh salads and wraps; dinner adds flank steak, shrimp skewers, mussels, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

data.location: 7808 Rea Rd Suite E, Charlotte, NC 28277
data.short_description: Article text is cut off before providing additional details beyond the address. No opening date or timeline provided in the available excerpt.

data.location: 109 S Main St, Monroe, NC 28112

data.short_description: A sit-down full-service Italian restaurant from Frank and Marcella Siano seating about 40 guests, distinct from their Siano's Pizza and Pasta in Waxhaw. Menu features house-made pasta, lasagna, seafood dishes, and table-side Pecorino cheese wheel service.

Known for blue crabs and their trademarked Stutter Butter sauce; William Grant and son Spencer will operate the Havana location.

data.location: 23 W Hibiscus Blvd, Melbourne, FL 32901
data.short_description: The company's sixth location, continuing the family-run bagel concept that opened its first store in Satellite Beach about five years ago, owned by Bernard and Elizabeth Corrigan.

data.location: 930 Blanding Blvd., Suite D & E, Orange Park, FL 32065
data.short_description: Owned by Katzumi Yonekura and Juan Yonekura, building on the success of their existing Gochas Empanadas food truck which has a following in Jacksonville. Target opening is early May 2026.

data.short_description: Article text is cut off before providing the full address or opening timeline. Collier County, Naples, FL location.

data.short_description: A boutique upscale wine bar concept from father-daughter team Michael Surapine and Bailey Surapine-Meen centered around a global wine experience.

data.location: 7125 Medley Crossing, Johns Creek, GA 30097

data.location: Metro Atlanta, GA (mobile trailer — no fixed address)
data.short_description: A mobile plant-based vegan dessert trailer from owner Tonya Whitfield serving West End, Atlantic Station, Piedmont Park, and Grant Park. Menu focuses on dairy-free, allergy-friendly frozen treats including organic fruit-based pops and non-dairy ice cream.

data.location: 7125 Medley Crossing, Johns Creek, GA 30097
data.short_description: The brand's third Georgia location within the Medley mixed-use development, franchised by German Rodriguez, offering gelato, macarons, crepes, waffles, hot chocolate, coffee, and affogatos. Indoor and outdoor seating along the green space; open daily with extended weekend hours.

data.location: Metro Atlanta, GA (mobile trailer — no fixed address)
data.short_description: A mobile vegan dessert trailer from owner Tonya Whitfield focused on clean-label, dairy-free frozen treats made without artificial dyes or preservatives. Plans include a potential soft opening before May 1 as initial trailer placement locations are confirmed.

data.location: 1102 Trust Ave Ste 300, Augusta, GA 30909

data.short_description: A Tex-Mex franchise location just off Wheeler Road, owned by franchisee Madison Tuten who also owns another Surcheros off Washington Road in Augusta. Tuten also plans to open additional locations in Evans, GA and Aiken, SC in Q1 2027.

data.location: 2850 Paces Ferry Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339
data.status: will likely open in several months
data.short_description: Taking over the former Stockyard space in Vinings, bringing a second location of the existing Gainesville concept with planned front-of-house renovations. Offers upscale Italian dining with weekend lunch/dinner and weekday dinner service.

data.location: 29 West Park Square NE, Marietta, GA 30060
data.status: targeting an opening the week of April 20
data.short_description: An upscale two-level sports bar in Marietta Square with a "dark and moody" upstairs (~70 seats), a downstairs bar (~25 seats), and one of the larger patios in the area. Menu centers on elevated bar fare including oysters, wings, and burgers.

data.location: 523 West King Street, Boone, NC 28607
data.event_date: Approximately two weeks from article date (targeted)

Delayed by hurricane-related construction setbacks; planned hours Mon–Thu 1–9 p.m., Fri–Sat 12–10 p.m., Sun 12–7 p.m.

data.location: 319 N First Ave, Knightdale, NC 27545

data.status: preparing to open its first brick-and-mortar location
data.short_description: The brand's first permanent location, expanding from a mobile coffee cart, operating as a day-to-night coffee shop transitioning from morning café to evening social space with beer, wine, and THC beverages. Co-owned by Jonny Mocha and Cody Alexander Vaughn, centered on inclusivity and mental health awareness.

data.location: 2263 Cheshire Bridge Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30324
data.short_description: A second location of the French Toast brand, taking over the former Blu Lagoon space, known for breakfast, brunch, and lunch with an emphasis on French toast variations and service-forward dining. Open seven days a week: Mon–Fri 7 a.m.–3 p.m., Sat–Sun 6 a.m.–5 p.m.

data.location: 2180 W First St, Fort Myers, FL 33901
data.short_description: A large downtown brewery expansion with 17 taps featuring in-house lagers, IPAs, sours, and hard seltzers, with food provided by food trucks and nearby restaurants. Owned by Jordan Weisberg; planned daily 11 a.m.–11 p.m. with live music and trivia events.

data.location: 535 SE Baldwin Dr., Lee, FL 32059
data.short_description: A roaming food truck serving Lee County focused on scratch-made baked empanadas, quesadillas, and rotating specials, owned by Jose and Sandra Flores. Operating Wednesday–Saturday 10 a.m.–5 p.m.

data.location: 8156 Alico Road, Unit 10, Fort Myers, FL 33912
data.short_description: A hybrid coffee and botanical tea lounge combining daytime workspace energy with late-night social vibes, offering espresso drinks, kava and kratom teas, and light food. Founded by Tony Toska and operated locally by Derek Weimer; open early morning to late night with weekends until 2 a.m.

data.location: 5110 Pine Island Rd #140, Bokeelia, FL 33922
data.short_description: A brunch-forward restaurant with a modern interior seating around 50 guests, offering elevated brunch dishes, rotating specials, cinnamon rolls, and specialty coffee drinks. Owned by Erika Fernandez and Carla Arguello; exact hours TBD.

data.location: 4720 SE 9th Place, Ste 200, Cape Coral, FL 33904
data.short_description: Article text is truncated before providing status, opening date, or additional details for Big Nick's BBQ beyond the address.

data.location: 32 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
data.short_description: A modern coastal full-service restaurant with nearly 300 indoor and outdoor seats from Specialty Restaurants Corporation, led by owner John Tallichet, known for the Rusty Pelican concept. Menu centers on fresh seafood including a full raw bar, hot seafood tower, and lobster and shrimp risotto.

data.location: 12325 Northlake Blvd., Suite 130, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33412
data.short_description: A full-service gastropub with ~167 seats including a 22-seat bar, led by owner Nick Lanier alongside co-owners Tara Lanier, Brendon O'hara, and Joel Hartunian. Menu described as "rustic refined" featuring smash burgers, rotating oysters, raw bar, and a signature "Kentucky barbecue fried gator" dish by executive chef David Schroeder.

data.location: 3009 Ponce de Leon Blvd., Coral Gables, FL 33134
data.event_date: April 28, 2026; plans to be open by May 1, 2026 (targeted)
data.status: targeting an April 28 opening, with plans to be open by May 1
data.short_description: An indoor golf simulator, bar, and restaurant concept featuring ~175 seats across simulator bays, bowling lanes, and dining areas, serving as the Florida flagship for franchisees Peter McCormick, Alex Zega, and Matt Csillag. Also introduces Strike Club, a sister concept with 12 full-size bowling lanes.

data.location: 122 N. Dixie Highway, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
data.short_description: A fast-casual breakfast and lunch cafe with counter service and indoor/outdoor seating, owned by Chef Ethan Haim. Menu features egg sandwiches, omelets, Benedicts, smoothies, specialty French toast, wraps, burgers, and house-made soups; open Mon–Fri 6:30 a.m.–3 p.m., Sat 6:30 a.m.–2 p.m., Sun 10 a.m.–2 p.m.

data.location: 8801 Boynton Beach Blvd, Boynton Beach, FL 33472
data.short_description: A fast-casual concept focused on made-to-order fried chicken and seafood from a long-running family brand, owned by Phillip Brinkman and family. Menu features fresh chicken tenders, fried cod, shrimp, and clams; open daily 10:30 a.m.–9 p.m.

data.location: 405 Federal Highway, Lake Park, FL 33403
data.short_description: A full-service Italian American restaurant with a casual, homey setting and indoor/outdoor seating, owned by Monique Rabyor and Alfredo Verrone. Menu features house-made pasta, cacio e pepe, burrata, and antipasti; open Tue–Thu 11 a.m.–9 p.m., Fri–Sat 11 a.m.–10 p.m., Sun 4–9 p.m.

data.location: 1649 Forum Place, Suite 3, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
data.short_description: A casual Italian-American restaurant offering dine-in and takeout with a focus on consistency and customer experience. Menu includes pizza, wings, baked pastas, and entrees like chicken francese and veal; article text is truncated before additional details.

data.location: 550 Wells Rd Ste 1, Orange Park, FL 32073
data.status: targeting a late April or early May opening
data.short_description: A full-service restaurant and lounge with approximately 140 seats and a full liquor bar, owned by Pedro Parks and focused on pasta dishes, char-grilled oysters, flatbreads, and weekend brunch. Menu emphasizes linguine or scampi-style pasta paired with proteins like shrimp, chicken, salmon, or lobster.

data.location: 12553 Bartram Park Blvd., Suite 302, Jacksonville, FL 32258
data.status: targeting a June 18 opening, pending permits and construction
data.short_description: A specialty coffee franchise in fast-casual format with 25–30 indoor seats and a small patio, owned by Daniel and Nicole Price who were drawn to the brand during their time at Florida State University in Tallahassee. Menu centers on an Italian-style espresso program including lattes, cappuccinos, cold brew, and nitro cold brew.

data.location: 930 Blanding Blvd., Suite D & E, Orange Park, FL 32065
data.short_description: A fast-casual 16-seat Venezuelan restaurant from owners Katzumi and Juan Yonekura, building on their existing Gochas Empanadas food truck following in Jacksonville. Menu centers on scratch-made empanadas, cachapas, fresh juices, and breakfast offerings, with recipes from Yonekura's mother-in-law Sonia Barajas.
